title: Supreme Chancellor election, 40 BBY
content:
{{Top|leg}}
{{Conjecture|legends}}

|name=Supreme Chancellor election, 40 BBY
|date=[[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]]<ref name="NEC">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''</ref>

{{Quote|In 40 BBY, Finis Valorum began the first of two terms as Supreme Chancellor of the Republic.|A New Republic historical document|<ref name="NEC" />}}
The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Senate]] held an '''election''' for the role of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] in [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]]. [[Finis Valorum/Legends|Finis Valorum]] was the successful candidate, having risen to prominence through his actions in the [[Stark Hyperspace War]], he won through the recognition of his [[House Valorum/Legends|family's]] [[Politics/Legends|political]] legacy and the backing of influential families such as the [[Tarkin family/Legends|Tarkins]] and the [[Kalpana family|Kalpanas]]. Valorum later [[Supreme Chancellor election, 36 BBY/Legends|won re-election]] in [[36 BBY/Legends|36 BBY]], but was removed from office in a [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|Vote of No Confidence]] near the end of his second term.

==Prelude==
The [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] held its election for the office of [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] in the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[40 BBY/Legends|40 BBY]].<ref name="NEC" /> Prior to the election, [[Kalpana (Supreme Chancellor)/Legends|Kalpana]] had served as Chancellor.<ref name="CSWE">{{CSWECite|I|196|Kalpana, Supreme Chancellor}}</ref>

===Finis Valorum's candidacy===
{{Quote|The part Finis Valorum played made him a rising star in the Senate and contributed to his becoming Chancellor, although there was a political price paid. Ranulph Tarkin's family was rich, important, and powerful. Their price for supporting Valorum was to make Tarkin the 'Hero of Troiken.'|Jace Dallin|<ref name="SW39" />}}
[[File:FinisValorum-FF56.png|thumb|right|250px|Finis Valorum, the successful candidate of the election]]
Finis Valorum of the [[House Valorum/Legends|House Valorum]] [[Politics/Legends|political]] dynasty—which had included three previous [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellors]] of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]<ref name="Plagueis">[[Darth Plagueis (novel)|''Darth Plagueis'']]</ref>—began his political career in his late thirties,<ref name="Characters" /> and by [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]],<ref name="Valorum age">According to {{FactFile|56|VAL 1-4|Finis Valorum}}, Valorum was working as a [[Senator/Legends|senator]] approximately thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], which began in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]] according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''. Therefore, Valorum must have become a senator by around [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]].</ref> had become [[Senator/Legends|senator]] of the [[Lytton sector/Legends|Lytton sector]]. He ascended quickly through the [[Galactic Senate/Legends|Galactic Senate's]] hierarchy<ref name="Characters" /> and earned the attention of political pundits through his actions during the [[Stark Hyperspace War/Legends|Stark Hyperspace War]]<ref name="Fact File 56">{{FactFile|56|VAL 1-4|Finis Valorum}}</ref> of [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]],<ref name="NEC" /> during which he acted as a negotiator and convinced the Senate to resist conceding to the demands of the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Iaco Stark/Legends|Iaco Stark]].<ref name="SW39" />

To win the election, Valorum received the support of the wealthy and influential [[Tarkin family/Legends|Tarkin family]]<ref name="SW39" /> of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Eriadu/Legends|Eriadu]].<ref name="Plagueis" /> [[Ranulph Tarkin/Legends|Ranulph Tarkin]], <ref name="SW39" /> Valorum's former political adversary,<ref name="Plagueis" /> had intentionally [[Death/Legends|sacrificed]] himself on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Troiken/Legends|Troiken]] for his own glory at the [[Fifth Battle of Qotile|end]] of the Hyperspace War. The Tarkin family gave Valorum their support<ref name="SW39" /> on the condition that he decorate<ref name="NEC" /> Ranulph as the "Hero of Troiken."<ref name="SW39" /> Valorum also made deals with the [[Kalpana family]].<ref name="Plagueis" />

==The election==
Valorum won the election, although his victory was described as shallow and cynical. His allies only supported Valorum because he was considered the best prospect out of an unremarkable pool of candidates. The Republic population only knew of Valorum because of his famous surname and none of his other attributes.<ref name="Characters" /> Valorum believed that his position was secure at the time of his election.<ref name="Fact File 56" /> The [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Darth Plagueis/Legends|Darth Plagueis]] believed that the return of a Valorum to the Chancery was a positive sign for the [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] [[Grand Plan/Legends|Grand Plan]]. His [[Sith apprentice/Legends|apprentice]], [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]], who was publicly only known as Senator Palpatine of the planet [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]], believed that Valorum's election had more to do with his family legacy and the support of influential families than his own diplomatic skill.<ref name="Plagueis" /> This sentiment was echoed by Valorum's enemies.<ref name="Databank">{{DB|character|finisvalorum|Valorum, Supreme Chancellor Finis}}</ref>

==Aftermath==
As the new Chancellor, Valorum declared Ranulph Tarkin posthumously as the "Hero of Troiken," which won further backing for Tarkin's ideology of [[Militarists/Legends|militarism]], which advocated for the creation of a [[Grand Army of the Republic/Legends|Republic Grand Army]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref> The election was supposed to be the pinnacle of Valorum's political success,<ref name="Insiders">[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']]</ref> yet he found his time in office beset with obstacles.<ref name="Fact File 56" /> Valorum won [[Supreme Chancellor election, 36 BBY/Legends|re-election]] again in [[36 BBY/Legends|36 BBY]]<ref name="NEC" /> by a slim margin.<ref name="Characters" /> Near the end of his second term<ref name="Plagueis" /> in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], Valorum was ousted from office through a [[Vote of No Confidence/Legends|Vote of No Confidence]] engineered by Palpatine, who [[Supreme Chancellor election, 32 BBY/Legends|succeeded]] Valorum as Chancellor.<ref name="NEC" /> In [[30 BBY/Legends|30 BBY]], the Tarkin family's role in electing Valorum was mentioned by Stark Hyperspace War veteran [[Jace Dallin]] in a recounting of the war to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]] [[Aayla Secura/Legends|Aayla Secura]].<ref name="SW39" />

==Behind the scenes==
Finis Valorum's successful election as Supreme Chancellor was first mentioned in the [[1999]] [[LucasArts]] CD-ROM ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide]]''.<ref name="Insiders">[[Star Wars: Episode I Insider's Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Insider's Guide'']]</ref>
